title: FOUNDATION-CUT output — foundational decisions, seams, invariants, deferred items

skeleton_ref.description: Path to 04-skeleton.json providing the walking skeleton and seams.

sequence_ref.description: Path to 05-sequence.json providing the ordered slice list.

foundation_cut.description: The three lists comprising the foundational cut: decisions, seams, and cross-slice invariants.

foundational_decisions.description: Decision categories Phase 2 must resolve; each needed before skeleton or first dependent slice can be built.

id.description: FD* identifier for this foundational decision.

category.description: What Phase 2 must resolve — named but not decided; no vendor/stack/library named.

needed_by.description: S* IDs of slices that require this decision to be resolved first.

why_foundational.description: Why decided once before slicing — skeleton or every slice needs it; cannot be deferred.

grounded_in.description: aPRD IDs that exist verbatim and ground this decision.

skeleton_seams.description: Carried from 04-skeleton.json, one per present seam, wording refinable; no seam added or dropped.

must_establish.description: Functional touch-point skeleton must prove; named-not-designed, no component/stack/vendor.

cross_slice_invariants.description: Cross-cutting properties holding across all slices; first entry always INV-ECON engine default; rest aPRD-derived only.

id.description: INV-ECON (engine default) or INV* identifier for this invariant.

invariant.description: Cross-slice aPRD-fixed property decided once; restates aPRD, never invented.

applies_to.description: Why this invariant holds across slices and which slices inherit it.

grounded_in.description: aPRD IDs (C*/A*/E* or engine canon) that ground this invariant.

deferred.description: HOW-decisions deliberately not in the cut; thinness evidence; every post-skeleton slice should be represented.

item.description: HOW-decision deliberately not in the foundation cut.

defer_to.description: S* identifier of the slice that will own this decision.

reason.description: Why deferring is safe — only this slice needs it; not cross-slice, not on skeleton path.

grounded_in.description: aPRD IDs that ground the deferral.

coverage.description: Coverage accounting: seams carried from 04, post-skeleton slices, and slices represented in deferred[].

post_skeleton_slices.description: Slice ids after position 1 in the 05-sequence.json order.

deferred_slices.description: Distinct defer_to S* ids in deferred[]; should cover post_skeleton_slices.

cut_counts.description: Integer counts of each cut list for quick verification.
